name: engineering-workspace
description: Structured, visual SDLC workflow management for AI-assisted development in Code Studio.

chatParticipants.name: engineering
chatParticipants.description: Manage structured SDLC workflows for AI-assisted development.

languageModelTools.name: engineering_setup_project
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Initializes the .codestudio/ directory with project context (config.json, context.md). Scans the workspace to detect languages, frameworks, test frameworks, and conventions. Use when the user wants to set up a project for AI-assisted engineering workflows, or when .codestudio/ doesn't exist yet. After this tool runs, create a codestudio-instructions.md file with the project's coding conventions, then call engineering_start_workflow with the user's objective to begin the SDLC workflow.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Initialize Engineering Workspace for this project

languageModelTools.name: engineering_start_workflow
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Starts a structured SDLC workflow. YOU (the agent) provide the assessment — workType, complexity, riskLevel — based on your understanding of the project and objective. Do NOT rely on keyword matching. Creates workflow stages and activates the first stage. After calling this tool, follow the stage's skill: DEFINE → spec-driven-development skill then engineering_save_artifact(type='spec'). PLAN → planning-and-task-breakdown skill then engineering_save_artifact(type='plan'). BUILD → incremental-implementation + test-driven-development skills. VERIFY → run tests then engineering_save_artifact(type='report'). REVIEW → code-review-and-quality skill then engineering_save_artifact(type='review'). SHIP → shipping-and-launch skill.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Start a structured engineering workflow for a task

objective.description: What the user wants to build — the work request objective

workType.description: Type of work — you determine this based on the objective

complexity.description: Estimated complexity — you determine this based on scope and risk

riskLevel.description: Risk level — you determine this based on what the task touches (auth, payments, data, etc.)

processLevel.description: Optional. Process level override. If not provided, inferred from riskLevel + complexity. light=trivial fixes, standard=normal features, thorough=architecture/security, guarded=migrations/auth changes

contextSignals.description: Optional. Context signals you detected: touches_ui, touches_api, touches_auth_or_input, touches_external_services, performance_sensitive, high_risk_decision

languageModelTools.name: engineering_save_artifact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Saves a workflow artifact (spec, plan, review, or report) to the .codestudio/workflows/current/artifacts/ directory. Use after generating content with a skill: after spec-driven-development → save type='spec'. After planning-and-task-breakdown → save type='plan'. After code-review-and-quality → save type='review'. After running tests → save type='report'. The artifact will appear in the Engineering Workspace sidebar for user review and approval. After saving, call engineering_advance_stage to move to the next stage.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Save a spec, plan, review, or report to the workflow

type.description: Artifact type: spec (specification), plan (task breakdown), review (code review), report (verification report)

content.description: The full markdown content of the artifact

stage.description: Which workflow stage produced this artifact

languageModelTools.name: engineering_advance_stage
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Checks if the current workflow stage requirements are met (required artifacts saved, quality gates passing, approvals granted) and advances to the next stage. Returns the next stage's instructions. Use after completing the work for the current stage. If requirements are not met, returns what's still needed.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check requirements and advance to the next workflow stage

force.description: Force advance even if some non-blocking requirements are not met

languageModelTools.name: analyze_work_request
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Analyzes a work request objective to determine work type, complexity, risk level, risk signals, and recommended process level (light/standard/thorough/guarded). Use when you need to understand the risk profile of a task without starting a full workflow.

objective.description: The work request objective to analyze.

languageModelTools.name: get_workflow_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the current engineering workflow state including active stage, completed/pending stages, progress percentage, and any pending approvals.

includeHistory.description: Include recent history entries in the response.

languageModelTools.name: get_project_context
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the auto-generated project context including detected languages, frameworks, test frameworks, package managers, build tools, and conventions.
